verbatim_text: In this case, we know that the user is only into basketball. And by taking the dot product of these two vectors, we can derive the user's preference score on this article. OK, so we're keeping track of a distribution over thetas. OK, so we're keeping track of a distribution over thetas, which is our user model. And we're going to do belief updates through posterior inference. So we want to estimate the posterior probability of a user preference after observing a question O and a user answer I. We can do this by Bayes' rule. And we're going to estimate this with a particle filter. After we update our belief about the user, we want to select the most informative question to ask next. We do this by searching over questions and selecting the one that maximizes information gain about user preferences theta. So we want to take the question that maximally reduces our uncertainty about theta. OK, so we want to measure the effect of doing each of these steps either implicitly inside of the language model or externally with a Bayesian model. So we're going to isolate the effect of doing either both of these steps with a Bayesian model, one with a language model, one with a Bayesian model, or both of them with the language model. On the y-axis again, we have the accuracy of the final specification as measured by how well we can predict the user's choice on held out instances.
